activity_id: Integer: Required: Activity identifier. 1 = Log, 2 = Collect, 99 = Other.
type_uid: Integer: Required: Event type ID. 500101 = Log, 500102 = Collect, 500199 = Other.
severity_id: Integer: Required: Severity level. 1 = Informational, 2 = Low, 3 = Medium, 4 = High, 5 = Critical.
time: Timestamp: Required: Time this inventory event occurred in ISO 8601 format.
message: String: Recommended: Description of this device inventory event as defined by the source.

device.hostname: String: Required: Primary hostname of the endpoint.
device.uid: String: Recommended: Unique identifier assigned to the device by the source system e.g. Windows SID, AWS EC2 ARN, Jamf serial number.
device.name: String: Optional: Alternate device name ordinarily as assigned by an administrator.
device.ip: String: Recommended: Primary IP address of the endpoint.
device.type: String: Optional: Endpoint platform or form factor e.g. Workstation, Laptop, Server, Mobile, VM.

os.name: String: Optional: Operating system name e.g. Windows, Linux, macOS.
os.version: String: Optional: Operating system version or build e.g. 11.0.22631, 22.04.
os.latest_version: String: Optional: Latest available version of the operating system. Used to determine if device is up to date.

org.name: String: Optional: Name of the organization or tenant the endpoint belongs to.

device.agent_list: Array: Optional: A list of agent objects associated with a device endpoint or resource.
device.interface_name: String: Recommended: Name of the network interface e.g. eth1.
device.interface_uid: String: Recommended: Unique identifier of the network interface.
device.os_machine_uuid: String: Optional: UUID of operating system assigned Machine ID.
device.is_managed: Boolean: Optional: Indicates whether the endpoint is managed by corporate tooling. True = managed, False = unmanaged.
device.created_time: String: Optional: Time when the device was first seen in ISO 8601 format.
device.last_seen_time: String: Optional: Last time the device checked in or reported telemetry in ISO 8601 format.

security.disk_encryption: Boolean: Recommended: Indicates whether full disk encryption is enabled e.g. FileVault on macOS, BitLocker on Windows, LUKS on Linux.
security.security_update: String: Recommended: Identifier of the latest OS security update or patch level applied to this endpoint e.g. OS build number or last installed KB/patch ID.
security.security_update_history_time: String: Optional: Timestamp of the most recent security update in ISO 8601 format.
security.patch_update: String: Recommended: Current OS patch compliance status e.g. Up to date, Outdated, Critical missing, Unknown.
security.password_policy: Boolean: Recommended: Indicates whether the endpoint complies with the organisation password policy.

status: String: Recommended: Current operational status of the endpoint e.g. Online, Offline, Retired.
status_id: Integer: Recommended: Normalized status code. 0 = Unknown, 1 = Success, 2 = Failure, 99 = Other.
status_detail: String: Recommended: Additional context describing the current status or reason for non-compliance.
status_code: String: Recommended: Status code as reported by the event source.

metadata.version: String: Required: OCSF schema version e.g. 1.0.0.
metadata.uid: String: Optional: Unique identifier of this specific inventory record. If no relevant ID exists keep blank.
metadata.logged_time: String: Optional: Time this record was logged in ISO 8601 format.

product.name: String: Required: Name of the platform this data came from e.g. Jamf, Intune, CrowdStrike. Write the name directly, not a variable.
product.vendor_name: String: Optional: Name of the vendor that owns the platform.

custom_attributes.comments: Array: Optional: Array of objects with fields author, timestamp, text representing analyst comments.
custom_attributes.tlp: Integer: Optional: Traffic Light Protocol classification. Default: 2.
custom_attributes.custom_fields: Object: Optional: Arbitrary key-value pairs for platform-specific or workflow-specific fields.
custom_attributes.assignee: String: Optional: Email or identifier of the analyst responsible for reviewing this endpoint.
